title: 'I don't know': Kapil Dev declines to comment on Vaibhav Sooryavanshi
content: Former Indian all-rounder Kapil Dev declined to comment on teenage IPL star Vaibhav Sooryavanshi, saying experts like Sunil Gavaskar and Ravi Shastri are better suited to judge him. The 15-year-old Rajasthan Royals opener has scored 404 runs in IPL 2026, including a 36-ball century, earning widespread praise and India call-up discussions.

title: As Accenture rolls out Microsoft Copilot to staff, CEO says teams doing higher-value work
content: Microsoft's Copilot 365 AI assistant is now powering Accenture's workforce of 743,000 employees, marking the largest enterprise deal for the chatbot. This significant adoption by Accenture, a leading AI adopter, highlights Microsoft's strategy to boost paid Copilot subscriptions. Early results show employees completing routine tasks up to 15 times faster, with major productivity gains reported.

title: India dismisses Nepal’s Kailash yatra objection over Lipulekh Pass
content: Nepal has officially objected to India resuming the Kailash Mansarovar Yatra through Lipulekh Pass, a territory it claims based on the 1816 Sugauli Treaty. India dismissed these claims as unjustified and historically unfounded, stating Lipulekh has been a long-standing yatra route. This dispute marks a potential test for bilateral ties as both nations prepare for diplomatic discussions.

title: Today's results to 5 Houses will give broad mood of the nation
content: Assembly poll results in West Bengal, Tamil Nadu, Assam, Kerala, and Puducherry will offer a glimpse into the national political mood. The outcomes could significantly impact the opposition INDIA bloc, with key contests in Bengal and Tamil Nadu holding particular importance for regional giants DMK and TMC.

title: ‘I'm vegetarian, need to get my protein’: When Google CEO talked about his breakfast
content: Google CEO Sundar Pichai revealed his morning habits. He wakes early, reads news, and enjoys a vegetarian breakfast. Pichai is particular about his tea, describing it as very English. He exercises later in the day. Pichai also shared his favorite Indian dishes from Bengaluru, Delhi, and Mumbai. He enjoys Dosa, Chola Bhatura, and Pav Bhaji respectively.

title: US goes ahead with radar upgrade for Pakistan’s F-16 fleet, move a worry for India
content: The US is proceeding with a $488 million radar upgrade for Pakistan's F-16 fighter jets, a move expected to heighten Indian security concerns. This program, contracted to Northrop Grumman, will enhance Pakistan's air capabilities amidst regional tensions. India's own fighter squadron numbers are reportedly below the required strength for a two-front conflict, making this development particularly noteworthy.

title: Bengal SIR a ‘threat’ to our jobs: Professionals, government staff move court
content: Livelihoods are at stake as 27 lakh voters in Bengal are stripped of voting rights due to electoral roll deletions during SIR. Professionals, including a PSU officer, teacher, doctor, and insurance agent, have moved the Calcutta High Court, fearing their citizenship will be questioned, potentially leading to job termination and licence cancellation. Their appeals for urgent hearings were denied.

title: 'Dhurandhar 2' BO collection day 45: Ranveer Singh’s film eyes Rs 1790 cr globally
content: Ranveer Singh's 'Dhurandhar 2' continues its phenomenal run, crossing Rs 1,138.54 crore in India net collections after 45 days. The film's 7th Saturday saw a significant surge, earning Rs 2.40 crore, pushing its gross worldwide total to Rs 1,788.32 crore. This success comes despite new releases, proving its enduring appeal.

title: Department of Justice sues $5B tech firm for excluding Americans from high-paying jobs
content: The US Department of Justice is suing tech firm Cloudera for allegedly discriminating against American job seekers. Prosecutors claim the company created a sham hiring process, favoring temporary visa holders for lucrative roles and actively deterring US workers. This lawsuit highlights the DOJ's commitment to protecting domestic employment opportunities.

title: SG Tushar Mehta takes swipe at ‘bully’ judges in his book
content: Senior advocate Tushar Mehta's upcoming book criticizes some judges for exhibiting 'judicial bullying' due to excessive courtroom reverence. He argues that while judges face immense pressure, this cannot justify incivility, as the public expects high standards from an institution they cannot easily remove. Mehta highlights the power imbalance lawyers face when confronting arrogant judges.

title: I am one of Brij Bhushan's six victims, reveals wrestler Vinesh Phogat
content: Wrestler Vinesh Phogat revealed she is one of six accusers of former WFI chief Brij Bhushan Singh. Her return to competition in his stronghold, Gonda, raises concerns about fairness and potential bias in officiating. Phogat, now a legislator, demands transparency, holding the government accountable for any incidents. The WFI president assures safety and denies bias.

title: Assembly poll results 2026: A defining test for BJP, Cong & regional satraps
content: As vote counting looms for five key states, the 2026 assembly elections represent a critical juncture for national parties and regional strongholds. The BJP seeks geographic expansion, while the Congress fights for relevance. Regional forces face mounting pressure, making the outcome a test of India's political trajectory.

title: Assembly elections 2026: From East to South, key battlegrounds to watch out for
content: After the counting begins and trends trickle in, these seats offer early clues to momentum, alliance cohesion and voter sentiment that can ripple across regions. From prestige contests involving chief ministers to urban battlegrounds testing new entrants, the outcomes in these constituencies will help explain whether incumbency holds or fractures. They also reflect how local issues such as welfare delivery, identity, urban infrastructure and leadership credibility interact with wider political narratives.

title: Admiral Tripathi meets Myanmar naval leadership, discusses steps to boost cooperation
content: Indian Navy Chief Admiral Dinesh K Tripathi concluded a visit to Myanmar, reinforcing defence ties and navy-to-navy cooperation. Discussions focused on enhancing training exchanges and operational engagements, including the handover of Indian-assisted maritime security equipment. This collaboration underscores India's strategic importance of Myanmar under its 'Neighbourhood First' policy.

title: Sai's 57, pacers help Gujarat Titans pull off tense win over Punjab Kings
content: Gujarat Titans edged past Punjab Kings by four wickets in a thrilling IPL clash. Sai Sudharsan anchored the chase with 57, while Washington Sundar stayed unbeaten on 40 to seal victory in the final over. Earlier, debutant Suryansh Shedge scored a fighting 57 for Punjab after early damage caused by Mohammed Siraj, Jason Holder and Kagiso Rabada.

title: Horoscope today: Daily astrological predictions for Monday
content: Horoscope Today: Read daily horoscope predictions for May 4, 2026. Know all about the astrological events and influences that will be affecting each of the 12 zodiac signs. Our astrologer has analyzed the movements of the planets and the alignment of the stars to bring you the most accurate and up-to-date horoscope predictions for the day ahead. Get ready to discover what the stars have in store for you today.

title: 'All officers of TMC': BJP raises red flag after phone found near strongroom; chaos erupts
content: A switched-off mobile phone and documents linked to the Raniganj constituency were found in an envelope near the Asansol Engineering College strong room, sparking a commotion. A BJP leader alleged bias from officers towards the TMC and raised concerns over potential irregularities. The incident occurred amidst heavy security for EVMs ahead of tomorrow's results.

title: 'He's a mouse': TMC takes shot at 'Singham' cop over repolling in Falta
content: Following serious accusations of election fraud in West Bengal's Falta area, Trinamool Congress heavyweight Kunal Ghosh is demanding that the controversial IPS officer, Ajay Pal Sharma, popularly known as 'Singham', be suspended. Ghosh believes Sharma's actions are directly linked to the alleged rigging that has cast a shadow over the election results.

title: Why it's no win for Congress even if DMK+ wins in Tamil Nadu
content: A victory for the Dravida Munnetra Kazhagam(DMK)-led front would be read primarily as an endorsement of the ruling party’s leadership. Chief minister MK Stalin remains the central figure in the state’s political battle, and governance credit is expected to accrue largely to his party. For Congress, which continues as a junior partner in the alliance, such an outcome would ensure representation and continuity, but not necessarily greater political ownership.

title: Deadly rally, deleted video, 90L 'ghosts' & fish: Inside the high-octane chaos of 2026 poll campaign
content: The 2026 Indian Assembly Elections showcased a nation in transition, marked by regional pride, welfare economics, and celebrity influence. Tragedies, controversial rhetoric, and mass voter roll deletions defined campaigns across states, leading to high voter turnout and intense political theatre.

title: IPL: Gujarat Titans go miles ahead in this record, no team comes close
content: Gujarat Titans are leading IPL 2026 with their pacers. They are taking the most wickets using short balls. Their fast bowlers effectively used bounce and hard lengths against Punjab Kings. Mohammed Siraj, Jason Holder, and Kagiso Rabada dismantled the top order. Holder took four wickets for 24 runs. Gujarat Titans are winning matches and changing how pace dictates games.

title: Seats low, stakes high: Is BJP expanding quietly in Kerala?
content: The Bharatiya Janata Party is making inroads in Kerala, challenging the state's traditional two-front political system. Recent elections show the BJP gaining ground in specific constituencies, turning contests into triangular battles. This shift is evident in Lok Sabha and local body polls, indicating a gradual disruption of the established LDF-UDF dominance. The party focuses on select seats for strategic growth.

title: Man United legend Sir Alex Ferguson hospitalised ahead of Liverpool clash
content: Former Manchester United manager Sir Alex Ferguson was admitted to hospital on Sunday after feeling unwell ahead of the club’s Premier League clash against Liverpool FC at Old Trafford. ​​The 84-year-old football legend, who still regularly attends Manchester United matches both home and away, had arrived at the stadium to watch one of English football’s fiercest rivalries when the health scare occurred.

title: Israel doubles air force fleet with mega US jet deal: What is 'Shield of Israel' plan?
content: Israel's defense ministry has approved a multi-billion dollar deal to purchase two new combat squadrons of advanced F-35 and F-15IA fighter jets from Lockheed Martin and Boeing. This significant expansion of air power aims to bolster the country's long-term military capability and air superiority, with a focus on domestic production and technological advancement.

title: WFI refuses to shift venue linked to Brij Bhushan despite Vinesh Phogat's concerns
content: "If Vinesh is worried about her safety, let me assure her that I am taking personal guarantee of that. Also, we have UWW-approved referees who officiate and all trial bouts are recorded, so there is no chance of any bias," Sanjay Singh was quoted as saying by news agency PTI.

title: How a weaker dollar is quietly making life more expensive for Americans
content: A declining U.S. dollar, down approximately 10% since Donald Trump's return to the White House, is quietly increasing the cost of everyday life for Americans. This weakening currency shrinks purchasing power, making imported goods and foreign travel more expensive, while benefiting large multinational exporters.

title: Watch: Diljit confronts fans waving pro-Khalistan flags, halts Canada concert
content: Diljit Dosanjh paused his Calgary concert amid pro-Khalistan flags, addressing criticism. He defended his efforts to highlight Punjab's issues on platforms like Kaun Banega Crorepati and The Tonight Show, stating his focus remains on his homeland. This follows past controversy with Sikhs For Justice over his interactions with Amitabh Bachchan.

title: Chakravarthy, Narine script SRH collapse as KKR end Hyderabad's winning run
content: Varun Chakravarthy took 3 for 36 and Sunil Narine claimed 2 for 31 as SRH were bowled out for 165 in 19 overs. KKR chased the target with 10 balls remaining to register their third straight win.

title: 'Impossible military op or ...': Iran mocks US, says Trump has only 2 options left
content: Iran's Revolutionary Guard Corps stated the US faces a choice between an "impossible" military operation or a "bad deal," as diplomatic efforts have stalled. Tehran reportedly submitted a 14-point proposal to Pakistan, outlining a 30-day framework to resolve issues, including US troop withdrawal and sanctions relief.

title: Manish Pandey scripts history, becomes only third player after Kohli & Rohit to ...
content: The 36-year-old replaced Ramandeep Singh in the KKR XI for their ninth match of the season. He had been bought for Rs 75 lakh at the auction and had not played earlier in the season.

title: What will TVK's Vijay be: Kejriwal of Delhi or Kishor of Bihar?
content: Actor-turned-politician Vijay Thalapathy's new party, TVK, has entered Tamil Nadu's political arena, aiming to disrupt the long-standing duopoly of DMK and AIADMK. While exit polls suggest a notable debut, projections vary, with some indicating TVK could act as a spoiler by splitting anti-incumbency votes.

title: Laura Loomer warns Candace Owens of lawsuit in Erika Kirk row
content: Candace Owens is facing a potential lawsuit threat from Laura Loomer after refusing to retract claims involving Erika Kirk and the 2025 killing of Charlie Kirk. Owens insists she stands by her statements, while Loomer warned legal action is imminent. The dispute has intensified with a separate lawsuit filed by Brian Harpole, escalating tensions.

title: 'Govt will be responsible': Phogat wary of competing at venue under Brij Bhushan influence
content: In a video message ahead of her return after nearly 18 months, Vinesh said the competition, to be held at a venue linked to Brij Bhushan Sharan Singh, could see results influenced by people close to the former Wrestling Federation of India (WFI) chief.

title: All set for counting; ECI promises transparency
content: Heavy security as counting of votes polled for elections to Assemblies of Assam, Kerala, Tamil Nadu, West Bengal and Puducherry and  bye-elections in seven Assembly constituencies of five States to be taken up

title: Tallest underwater human stack formed; Andaman sets two Guinness World Records in 2 days
content: Fourteen people, including Lieutenant-Governor D.K. Joshi, took part in forming the 22.3-metre-long stack, and they stayed under the water for three minutes, officials said

title: Saini urges Punjab OBCs to back BJP in Assembly polls
content: Addressing an OBC Sammelan in Rupnagar, Haryana CM Nayab Saini said the BJP ensures equal representation

title: Independent journalism punished, compliance rewarded under current regime, says Kharge
content: Sections of the media have been reduced to echoing the ruling establishment, while those who persist in asking questions are ‘targeted relentlessly’, says the Congress president while calling for deep introspection by all stakeholders on the occasion of World Press Freedom Day

title: Assembly elections 2026 LIVE: All set for counting, ECI promises transparency
content: The Chief Electoral Officer said 53,984 postal ballots had been issued to service voters, of which 20,028 had been received as of May 1.

title: Prohibitory orders imposed in Doda as AAP MLA returns home district after 8 months in jail
content: The lone AAP legislator in the Union Territory was arrested on September 8, 2025, following his ‘inappropriate remarks’ against then-Deputy Commissioner Harvinder Singh over the shifting of a health centre at Doda’s Kencha, and was booked under the Public Safety Act that allows preventive detention up to two years

title: Electoral debuts in Tamil Nadu’s past Assembly elections tell a mixed story of breakthroughs, missed chances
content: With the high profile debut of actor-politician C. Joseph Vijay’s Tamilaga Vettri Kazhagam in the latest polls, expectations are high about whether the party can breach the Dravidian duopoly or merely dent its margins

title: BC communities hopeful of political reservations in local body elections
content: BC Dedicated Commission Chairman Rajiv Ranjan Mishra, who has been appointed to ensure political reservations for all communities in accordance to their population, has visited three North Andhra districts

title: India rejects Nepal’s objection to Kailash Manasarovar Yatra via Lipulekh Pass
content: India remains open to a “constructive interaction” with Nepal on all issues in the bilateral relationship, including on boundary issues through “dialogue and diplomacy”, the Ministry of External Affairs said

title: High cost of five kg FTL cylinders keeps new consumers away
content: Consumers say a new five kg FTL connection costs ₹1,760, which is very high, and the cylinder will not last for 41 days unlike the regular cylinder

title: Velankanni priest says Basilica was not informed about TVK chief Vijay’s plan to visit shrine
content: The priest clarified that the morning schedule of prayers was not disrupted. He said devotees had expressed concern and anguish over the incident, prompting the church administration to take up the matter with the district administration and the police.

title: Vijay’s Velankanni visit cancelled; visual of supporters chanting ‘TVK’ inside Basilica goes viral
content: Addressing reporters at the party office in Karattur near Gobichettipalayam, Sengottaiyan said TVK party president Vijay is to emerge as a decisive leader and that the 2026 verdict will be historic

title: Tamil Nadu Assembly election 2026: Women make up over 80% of the additional voters
content: Of about 25.3 lakh more votes polled this time than in 2021, around 20.8 lakh came from women, the highest in the last four Assembly elections

title: ‘Kashmir will never be Pakistani and we will never become Pakistan’, says  Farooq Abdullah
content: “I pray that I will not die till I see the revival of Gandhi’s philosophy again”, says National Conference president

title: BJP closing seminaries with the help of J&K government: Mehbooba Mufti
content: The Peoples Democratic Party (PDP) president says Omar Abdullah government remained silent as Siraj-ul-Uloom in Shopian in south Kashmir was closed

title: Tamil Nadu | UPSC to shortlist DGP candidates on May 11
content: The new DGP/HoPF will have a minimum tenure of two years irrespective of date of superannuation, as per Supreme Court guidelines in the Prakash Singh case

title: Kerala Assembly polls 2026: LDF, UDF keeping their fingers crossed in Kozhikode
content: Once an LDF stronghold, the district may not witness a repeat performance, while UDF remains confident despite its lacklustre post-2001 performance; NDA expects to improve its vote share

title: Meeting takes stock of monsoon preparedness in Kozhikode district
content: Measures will be taken to ensure water sanitation, the safety of school buildings and the cleanliness of wells and drinking water tanks in schools

title: Counting of votes to be held at three centres in Ernakulam
content: Strong rooms will be opened at 6:30 a.m. in the presence of candidates or their authorised representatives, and counting will begin at 8 a.m. Postal ballots will be taken up first, followed by votes recorded in electronic voting machines

title: Ahead of Misri’s expected Nepal visit, Kathmandu expresses concern to India, China on Kailash yatra over Lipulekh
content: Nepal reiterates territorial claim to Lipulekh Pass, flags concerns over Kailash Manasarovar Yatra; India says the border pass has been long standing route for the yatra since 1954; it is unclear whether the Foreign Secretary will get to meet Nepal PM during the visit originally slated for May 11-12

title: Karnataka leadership talks in Delhi unlikely in next few weeks
content: Just as a group of Congress legislators aspiring to become Ministers are planning to reach Delhi next week to press for Cabinet expansion, the leadership issue in the Congress is unlikely to be settled soon

title: Results of bypolls to two Assembly seats in Karnataka on May 4
content: Amid rumblings in the Muslim voter base, the Congress government — just weeks short of turning three years —  is hoping to retain both Davangere South and Bagalkot constituencies, even as the BJP is confident of winning both

title: Assam election verdict: Congress hopes to stop BJP run
content: The BJP-led NDA is confident of a hat-trick of electoral wins, while the Congress-led alliance seeks to shed the Opposition tag

title: West Bengal waits with bated breath for May 4 Assembly election results
content: Counting will be held across 77 counting venues spread across 23 districts of the State; the Election Commission has deployed 432 counting observers (COs) to ensure transparent and smooth counting of votes on April 4.

title: J&K L-G galvanises massive public support against drug abuse, says neighbouring country using it for terrorism, radicalisation
content: On Mr. Sinha’s invitation, the Speaker of the House, Cabinet ministers, legislators, heads of regional parties, top security officials, religious leaders, educational institutes and administrators of hospitals attended a two-km-long rally

title: Jabalpur cruise tragedy death toll reaches 13; bodies of last two victims recovered
content: 28 people were rescued after a boat operated by M.P. tourism board capsized during a storm on April 30; high-level committee to probe the causes of the tragedy

title: Nishant launches Sadbhav Yatra from Bihar’s West Champaran
content: In Bettiah, Nishant Kumar follows his father Nitish Kumar’s long-held tradition of launching all yatras from Champaran

title: Father of murdered child demands death penalty for accused; opposition slams useless govt
content: The real accused is this Government, which is useless and incompetent; you lathi-charged the people who were carrying that child’s dead body, Raut said

title: ‘I am one of the six victims’, reveals Vinesh Phogat; opposes tourney at Brij Bhushan’s hometown
content: Making a startling revelation that she was among the six women wrestlers harassed by former WFI Wrestling Federation of India chief Brij Bhushan Sharan Singh, she says she won’t be able to give her ‘100%’ at the upcoming WFI ranking tournament

title: Hazrat Khwaja Bande Nawaz Urs gets under way in Kalaburagi
content: The 622nd Urs-e-Shareef of the revered Sufi saint draws thousands of devotees from across the country

title: Discuss and debate the Great Nicobar Project in Parliament: Congress
content: Jairam Ramesh says the government, rattled by Rahul Gandhi’s visit, had been forced to issue a clarification; it has failed to address serious ecological, tribal, financial and transparency concerns about the initiative, he said

title: TB dam crest gates repairs nearing completion, inauguration likely by May 15, says Shivakumar
content: Work to install the 33 crest gates has been completed, while slice plates have been fitted for 22 gates, according to the Deputy Chief Minister

title: Passenger from Sharjah detained after he opens emergency exit door, runs on tarmac after landing
content: As the aircraft was taxiing, the passenger opened the emergency exit door, came out through the evacuation slide, and ran on the tarmac towards gate no. 102, Chennai airport sources said.  As the flight was held up on the runway for nearly an hour, an international flight from London was diverted to Bengaluru

title: Trump Says US Will "Guide Ships" Safely Out Of Strait Of Hormuz
content: US President Donald Trump said Sunday that "very positive discussions" were under way with Iran on finding a solution to the crisis, but nevertheless added that the United States will soon start escorting ships through the blocked Strait of Hormuz.

title: 3 Die On Atlantic Cruise Ship From Suspected Hantavirus: WHO
content: Three people have died on a cruise ship in the Atlantic, the WHO said Sunday, one a confirmed case of hantavirus -- an illness usually transmitted to humans from rodents.

title: Iran Says No Nuclear Talks, Reviewing US Response To End War
content: Esmaeil Baqaei, the Foreign Ministry spokesperson, denied media claims about Iran's plan of suspension of Uranium enrichment, emphasising that Iran's plan is focused exclusively on ending the war, media reported on Sunday.

title: BJP Blitz vs Mamata Banerjee's Canny Politics: Bengal Results Today
content: The BJP's battle for Bengal, which had a quiet beginning in 2016, has accelerated over the years. From 3 seats and 10 per cent vote share in 2016, the party managed 77 seats and 38 per cent vote share by 2021 - a huge leap.

title: Hours Before Bengal Counting, Poll Body Clarification On VVPAT Slips Recovery
content: Several VVPAT slips, which ensure that a voter's vote has been registered in the voting machine, were found in a roadside drain at Madhyamgram in the district.

title: 5-Year-Old Killed In Delhi After 'Chinese Manjha' Slits His Throat
content: A five-year-old boy died after his neck was slit by banned 'Chinese manjha' while travelling with his family on a motorcycle in northeast Delhi's Usmanpur area on Sunday, police said.

title: Counting Of Votes For Bypolls In 7 Seats Across 5 States Today
content: Election results: Votes in these seats were cast on April 9 and April 23, along with the first and second phases of the assembly elections.

title: Kerala Assembly Election Results 2026: Kerala Counts Today With No Exit Poll Comfort For Ruling LDF
content: Kerala assembly election results 2026: The ruling LDF faces the counting day without the comfort of clear exit poll forecasts in its favour

title: BJP's Assam Fortress: Can Congress Exploit Anti-Incumbency In Key Seats?
content: Assam Assembly Election Results 2026: Polling in Assam was conducted in a single phase on April 9. A turnout of 85.96 per cent was recorded among the more than 2.50 crore registered voters.

title: Will N Rangaswamy Buck Puducherry Trend And Return To Power Again? 10 Points
content: The majority mark in the Union Territory is 16 and every seat counts - something that has been made more complicated with the entry of actor-politician Vijay's Tamilaga Vettri Kazhagam (TVK).

title: Why Nepal Is Objecting To India-China Kailash Mansarovar Yatra Plan
content: Nepal Foreign Ministry's statement said the government has drawn the attention of both India and China to its concerns through diplomatic channels.

title: New Video Exposes Systemic Safety Lapse Days Ahead Of Jabalpur Boat Tragedy
content: The video, shot barely 20 days before the fatal accident, shows senior district officials and elected representatives holding a meeting on the very same cruise boat that later sank in the dam.

title: IAS Academy Director Kidnapped, Forced To Transfer Rs 1.8 Crore At Gunpoint
content: Police say Priyank believed the academy director had substantial wealth and hatched the conspiracy to extort a large amount from her.

title: In Numbers: With Zero Deaths, Bengal Election Most Peaceful In 2 Decades
content: The 2026 Assembly elections have delivered an unprecedented shift: zero reported murders and no serious injuries, marking a significant break from this violent past.

title: New Evidence Proves White House Dinner Shooter Shot Secret Service Officer
content: Questions had lingered over whose weapon had struck the officer in his protective vest on the night of the April 25 gala, after at least one Secret Service agent also opened fire.

title: Day After 4 Children Found Killed In UP Home, Mother's Body Found In Drain
content: In a new development in the case involving the murder of four children inside their house in Uttar Pradesh's Ambedkar Nagar, police on Sunday fished out their mother's body from a drain.

title: 7 New Corridors, 65 Stations: Delhi Metro's Rs 48,000-Crore Expansion Plan
content: The Delhi Metro expansion is expected to extend metro connectivity deeper into the outskirts and rapidly developing parts of the city.

title: Cargo Ship Attacked By Multiple Small Vessels Near Hormuz Strait: UK Military
content: Iranian officials have asserted that they continue to control the strait and that ships not affiliated with the United States or Israel can pass if they pay a toll.

title: "Kept Calling For Help": Friends Recall Victims' Last Moments In Delhi Blaze
content: Grief and disbelief gripped family and friends of those killed in the devastating fire in east Delhi's Vivek Vihar on Sunday, with several recalling frantic early morning phone calls from the victims, desperate for help.

title: How Game-Changer Drishti Satellite Will Boost India's Space-Based Surveillance
content: The launch of the world's first OptoSAR satellite in India is a testament to our youth's passion for innovation and nation-building, PM Modi said today.
